data.title: Canada's Well-Below-Consensus Employment Points to A Bank of Canada Rate Cut Wednesday, Says Rosenberg Research
data.description:
Canada's Labour Force Survey (LFS) Friday surprised to the downside with a big miss, with employment up just 1,100 jobs — below consensus of 20,000 rises, which was already on the low side, said Rosenberg Research.

This confirms the weak labor market picture — lowest job creation number since July 2024 — and reinforces the Babj of Canada's easing bias, which supports a rate cut on Wednesday and beyond, noted Rosenberg Research. BoC rate cut odds for this week's policy meeting have gone from 50% the week before to 76% on Thursday and to 85% Friday.

Indeed, without wholesale trade hiring effects ahead of possible United States tariffs, the report would have shown an outright decline. This reverses the fairly strong employment picture investors saw in Q4 and is consistent with tariff uncertainty tipping Canada's growth downward, pointed out Rosenberg.

Unemployment was steady at 6.6% — well above the neutral unemployment rate of around 5.7%, continuing the excess supply picture. This was below consensus (6.7%) because of a drop in the participation rate to 65.3% from 65.5%, added Rosenberg. This continues to reinforce the BoC's dovish-but-slow approach.

Also showing the weak picture is the full-time employment decline (-20, 000), with the offset all coming from part-time work (+21, 000).

The employment picture varied "wildly" across industries, with wholesale and retail trade up 51,000, which Rosenberh believes partially reflects the impact of possible tariffs.

If wholesale and retail trade were omitted, there would have been a 50,000 job loss. Meanwhile, transportation and warehousing (-23, 000) and manufacturing (-5, 000) both fell, suggesting that the Canadian dollar loonie hasn't declined enough, as these are very currency-sensitive sectors. The goods-producing sector as a whole continued to decline (-20, 000) amid Canada's manufacturing recession, while services were up (+21, 000). Finance, insurance, and real estate (+16, 000) is a rate-sensitive area that continues to be a source of support as the BoC eases. Professional, scientific, and technical services (-33, 000) also saw a large drop.

Total hours worked fell 1.3% month over month due to snowstorms and other seasonal disruptions and were up just 0.5% year over year. This is a weak level consistent with limited business activity and a poor profitability picture.

Wage growth of 3.8% year over was slightly elevated from January's moderate read of 3.5%, but still consistent with a disinflating economy as it is the second-lowest year-over-year pace since May 2022. Like the drop in monthly hours worked, the month-over-month pop in wages is a result of the winter storms in February and should fade, according to Rosenberh. The build-in suggests an annualized employment growth in Q1 of +2.9%, mostly reflecting strong growth in January rather than last month.

Overall, everything in this report points to continued BoC easing.

data.title: Protagonist Therapeutics Shares Rise After Positive Trial Data for Icotronkinra
data.description:
By Chris Wack

Protagonist Therapeutics shares were up 28% to $49.25 after the company reported positive topline results from a Phase 2b study of icotrokinra, conducted by partner Johnson & Johnson.

The biopharmaceutical company said icotrokinra is a targeted oral peptide that selectively blocks the IL-23 receptor in adults with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis.

Protagonist said the study met its primary endpoint of clinical response in all icotrokinra dose groups evaluated. The study also demonstrated clinically meaningful differences versus placebo in key secondary endpoints of clinical remission, symptomatic remission and endoscopic improvement at week 12.

Icotrokinra had a response rate of 63.5% for patients treated with the highest dose at week 12, versus 27% for placebo. Clinical remission and response rates continued to improve through week 28.

Icotrokinra was well tolerated with the proportions of participants reporting one or more adverse events being similar between the icotrokinra dose groups and the placebo group.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: Gold Steady as Dollar and Treasury Yields Fall
data.description:
Gold was steady early on Monday as the dollar and treasury yields weaken.

Gold for April delivery was last seen down US$1.70 to US$2,912.40 per ounce.

The precious metal remains near a record high as U.S. President Donald Trump continues to unsettle markets as he imposes tariffs on the country's largest trading partners, spurring safe-haven demand for gold.

"Gold holds firm above USD 2,900 supported by concerns about the global economic outlook and haven demand amid tariffs and geopolitical tensions," Saxo Bank noted.

The dollar fell early, with the ICE dollar index last seen down 0.9 points to 103.75. Treasury yields also weakened, with the U.S. two-year note last seen paying 3.984%, down 5.2 basis points, while the yield on the 10-year note was down 4.9 points to 4.252%.

data.title: China Travel International Expects At Least 40% Lower Profit in 2024
data.description:
China Travel International Investment Hong Kong expects at least 40% lower attributable profit for 2024 than HK$240 million from 2023, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The travel and theme park company attributed the expected decrease to the decline in fair value of investment properties.

Shares of the company closed nearly 7% higher on Monday.

data.title: ArcelorMittal to temporarily halt production at its two main French plants
data.description:
ArcelorMittal on Monday announced a more than 270 million euro ($275.4 million) maintenance programme that would include the halt of activities at its plants in Dunkirk and Fos-sur-Mer, France, for several months.

From April 15, the world's second largest steelmaker will shut down operations for 90 days at its main blast furnace in Dunkirk, the largest in Europe, where it has already delayed planned green investments.

The cost of the maintenance operations in Dunkirk will be 254 million euros, while 18.3 million euros will be allocated for those in Fos-sur-mer, the company said.

($1 = 0.9222 euros)

data.title: White House official expects Q1 GDP to 'squeak' into positive category
data.description:
Key points:
  • Tariff uncertainty to be resolved in April, Hassett says
  • 'Blips in data' largely due to Biden's term, official says
  • Tax cuts to boost economy, trade just a small part, Hassett says

White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett on Monday said he expected uncertainty about President Donald Trump's trade policies to be resolved next month, and predicted first quarter GDP data would be positive, albeit slightly.

In an interview with CNBC, Hassett, who heads the National Economic Council, said there were many reasons to be bullish about the U.S. economy, despite some predictions of a negative GDP result in the first quarter and concerns about inflation.

Trump's tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico were already having the intended effect of bringing manufacturing and jobs back to the United States, he said.

"There are a lot of reasons to be extremely bullish about the economy going forward. But for sure, this quarter, there are some blips in the data, including the negative GDP, Dow, which are related both to the Biden inheritance and to some ... timing effects that are happening ahead of the tariffs," Hassett said.

He said the Atlanta Federal Reserve's prediction of negative GDP growth this quarter would be a "very temporary phenomenon," driven largely by a historical tendency to hold off on investment after a big election. This tendency should be resolved this month, and tariff uncertainty should be resolved in April, he said.

Reuters polls of economists last week showed risks to the Mexican, Canadian and American economies are piling up amid a chaotic implementation of U.S. tariffs that has created deep uncertainties for businesses and decision-makers. The surveys showed 70 of 74 economists polled across Canada, the U.S. and Mexico judged that the risk of a recession had increased.

Trump has imposed an additional 20% tariff on Chinese goods entering the United States, as well as 25% tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico, although he suspended most of the duties on U.S. neighbors until April 2, when he plans to unveil a global regime of reciprocal tariffs on all trading partners.

The seesaw tariff announcements have unnerved Wall Street - the U.S. benchmark S&P 500 index has given up all of its gains since Trump's November election.

Hassett struck an upbeat note, arguing U.S. tax cuts would boost the economy, increase investment and boost real wages by the second quarter, offsetting any negative fallout from the tariffs.

"Just be very wary ... of conversations about recession," he said. "What I think that what's going to happen is the first quarter is going to squeak into the positive category, and then the second quarter is going to take off as everybody sees the reality of the tax cuts," he said.

Hassett rejected the notion that consumers would bear the brunt of tariffs proposed and implemented, noting that having U.S. content would allow foreign producers to avert tariffs in many cases.

He noted that trade comprised only "a fraction" of the U.S. economy, and even if there was a small change in the price of imported goods, real wages were expected to go up as manufacturing employment rose.

data.title: UK's M&G gets approval for long-term asset fund
data.description: British insurer and asset manager M&G said on Monday its private markets business had received regulatory approval for its first long-term asset fund.

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1330 GMT on Monday:

** ServiceNow NOW.N said it would buy artificial intelligence company Moveworks for $2.85 billion, marking the software maker's largest-ever acquisition as it looks to expand its product portfolio and attract more customers.

** Rocket Companies RKT.N said it would acquire real estate listing platform Redfin RDFN.O in an all-stock deal valued at $1.75 billion, seeking to boost its lending business.

** Canada's oil producer Whitecap Resources WCP.TO will merge with peer Veren VRN.TO in an all-stock deal valued at C$15 billion ($10.43 billion) including debt, to form an energy giant with a larger footprint in the country's lucrative shale play.

** Broker consolidator PIB Group has expanded its MGA division with the acquisition of specialist litigation insurance MGA Litica.

** Sumitomo Mitsui Finance and Leasing (SMFL) (SUMFL.UL) said its joint venture will acquire Macquarie Rotorcraft, the UK-based helicopter leasing business of financial giant Macquarie's asset management arm.

** Britain's Alliance Pharma said it has agreed to a sweetened final cash proposal from asset management firm DBAY Advisors, valuing the healthcare group at about 362 million pounds ($467.5 million).

** Swiss private bank J. Safra Sarasin Group has agreed to buy 70% of Denmark's Saxo Bank in a deal valued at around 1.1 billion euro ($1.19 billion), Saxo Bank said.

** India's Sun Pharmaceutical Industries said it will acquire Checkpoint Therapeutics , a U.S.-based immunotherapy and targeted oncology company, for $355 million.

** Japan's Seven & i Holdings said that talks have begun with Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (ACT) over a store sale plan that would set the stage for ACT's $47 billion takeover bid.

data.title: Oil and gas firm Veren rises on proposed merger with Whitecap Resources in $10.4 billion deal
data.description:
** U.S.-listed shares of oil producer Veren , rise 18.3% to $5.81 premarket

** Co will merge with Whitecap Resources in an all-stock deal valued at C$15 billion ($10.43 billion) including debt, to form an energy giant

** Veren shareholders will receive 1.05 shares of Whitecap for each share held, or C$9.82 based on the last close, which implies a deal value of about C$6 billion, as per Reuters calculation

** It represents a 39% premium to Veren's close on Friday

** VRN shareholders will own 52% of the combined company, while WCP shareholders will own the rest 48%

** The combined company expects to have production of 370,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day, of which 63% will be liquids

** The transaction is expected to close before May 30, 2025

** As of last close, VRN shares were down 4.47% YTD

($1 = 1.4384 Canadian dollars)

About Oncimmune

Oncimmune is a precision medicine company, specialising in analysing immune interactions through the autoantibody profile. Taking a platform approach to generating insights, Oncimmune is partnering with global pharmaceutical and biotech companies, as well as contract research organisations (CROs) to discover novel biomarkers for the development of more targeted and effective therapies across many immune-mediated diseases. Our mission at Oncimmune is to enable precision medicine. We help our partners to discover novel biomarkers, drug targets and predict treatment efficacy through the application of our platform. We are able to do this by deploying our world class scientific team and our cutting-edge technology platform, built on years of experience in the field. Our aim is to make this an essential tool in drug discovery and development.

Oncimmune is headquartered in the UK, with its discovery and development facility based in Dortmund, Germany and a business development team based in the US and Europe.

For more information, visit www.oncimmune.com

data.title: Assura to Accept KKR's $2.08 Bln Takeover Offer — Update
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Assura said it started discussions on an improved takeover offer from the consortium formed by KKR and Stonepeak Partners that values the company at 1.61 billion pounds ($2.08 billion).

The London-listed property business on Monday said its board would recommend the deal if the 49.4 pence-a-share bid becomes a firm offer, and allowed the consortium to complete a due diligence process.

The consortium's offer represents a premium of nearly 32% to the shares' closing price of 37.4 pence on Feb. 13, and a 2.9% increase from KKR's previous proposal of 48 pence a share that was rejected on Feb. 18. This is the fifth indicative nonbinding proposal from KKR to the board of Assura.

Under the deal, Assura shareholders would keep the declared quarterly dividend of 0.84 pence a share and receive cash consideration of 48.56 pence a share at closing.

"The consortium of KKR and Stonepeak, both long-term infrastructure investors, recognizes that Assura's leading platform and portfolio are important social infrastructure assets for the U.K., and has indicated its intention to deploy further capital to the portfolio to continue its growth," the company said.

Assura added that it rejected an offer from Primary Health Properties regarding a possible all-share combination of Assura and PHP, as it considered KKR's offer to be more attractive because it provides cash at a significantly higher value per share and with materially less risk.

In a separate statement, Primary Health Properties said it is "considering its position" after confirming that it has made an unsuccessful preliminary approach. PHP added that it didn't have any details around the exchange ratio.

The company said a business combination would boost the scale of both companies' portfolio, reduce costs and realize significant value from synergies.

PHP has until April 7 to either announce a firm intention to make an offer or walk away.

Assura stock rose more than 14% on the news at 46.62 pounds, on track for a record intraday increase.

According to AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould, the current share price is below KKR's latest proposal, suggesting skepticism if the new KKR proposal is a done deal. It also implies the market doesn't believe Primary Health Properties is going to come back with a significantly better offer.

"The 31.9% bid premium is significantly below the 47% average for U.K. takeovers in 2024, meaning Assura's shareholders might feel they aren't being compensated adequately. After all, they would be giving up an investment that could generate much greater returns over time," Mould added.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: SEA Holdings Forecasts Narrower Loss in 2024
data.description:
SEA Holdings expects a loss of about HK$140 million for 2024, narrower than the HK$854 million loss from 2023, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The property investment and development company attributed the expected decrease to the lack of impairment loss on the listed debt securities investments and a reduction in fair value loss on investment properties in the United Kingdom.

data.title: Service Properties Trust Names Chris Bilotto CEO
data.description:
Service Properties Trust said Monday it has appointed Chris Bilotto as chief executive, managing trustee and president.

Bilotto is an executive vice president of The RMR Group and also serves as the CEO, managing trustee and president of Diversified Healthcare Trust , Service Properties Trust said.

Bilotto's appointment comes after the resignations of Todd Hargreaves and John Murray on March 9, the company said.

data.title: Trucking insurtech Nirvana closes $80 million Series C at near-$850 million valuation
data.description:
(The Insurer) - AI-driven commercial trucking insurtech Nirvana Insurance has closed an $80 million Series C funding round, valuing the company at nearly $850 million.

The round was led by General Catalyst with continued support from existing investors Lightspeed Venture Partners and Valor Equity Partners.

San Francisco-based Nirvana said the new round follows a year in which it exceeded $100 million in premiums, more than doubling year over year.

Nirvana previously raised $57 million in Series B funding in October 2023, which followed a $22 million Series A round in 2022.

The new investment will be used to expand Nirvana further and for continued technological advancements in commercial fleet insurance.

Nirvana fully integrates Internet of Things data that is embedded in telematics devices across fleets, and has analysed more than 20 billion miles of real-world driving.

"This investment allows us to scale our impact, ensuring fleets have access to personalised, real-time insurance solutions that reward safety – it's a fairer approach for the fleets, and a more profitable approach for an insurance carrier,” said Nirvana CEO Rushil Goel.

Nirvana in October last year announced a partnership with hybrid fronting carrier MS Transverse Insurance Company that it said provided a “boost in capacity”.

data.title: Full Truck Alliance Is Maintained at Equal-Weight by Barclays
data.description: (13:30 GMT) Full Truck Alliance Price Target Raised to $13.00/Share From $7.00 by Barclays

data.title: ServiceNow to Acquire Moveworks for $2.85 Billion
data.description:
ServiceNow said Monday it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Moveworks for $2.85 billion.

"The acquisition will combine ServiceNow's agentic AI and automation strengths with Moveworks' front-end AI assistant and enterprise search technology to unlock new experiences for every employee for every corner of the business," ServiceNow said in a press release.

The $2.85 billion deal, which is expected to close in the second half of the year, is subject to customary purchase price adjustments and is payable in a combination of cash and stock, ServiceNow said.
